key_points: No adverse reactions related to doxycycline combined with laser/light therapy were reported in 36 rosacea patients.
No photosensitivity or phototoxicity reactions were observed to pulsed dye laser (PDL) or intense pulsed light (IPL) in patients receiving concomitant systemic doxycycline.
Both standard-dose and anti-inflammatory dose doxycycline were used concurrently with laser/light treatments without safety concerns.
All patients achieved some degree of clearance of rosacea signs and symptoms.
The theoretical risk of photosensitivity from doxycycline combined with laser/IPL therapy was not observed in this retrospective cohort.
Combination therapy (doxycycline + laser/light) may yield better results than monotherapy for rosacea management.

outcomes.measured: Incidence of photosensitivity or phototoxicity reactions
outcomes.result: Zero adverse reactions related to photosensitivity observed in 36 patients
outcomes.clinical_significance: Supports safety of combining doxycycline with PDL or IPL for rosacea treatment

outcomes.result: All patients achieved some degree of clearance
outcomes.clinical_significance: Combination therapy appears effective for rosacea symptom reduction

notes: Retrospective study with small sample size (n=36). No control group. Results suggest safety but cannot definitively rule out rare photosensitivity events. Theoretical risk of doxycycline-induced photosensitivity with laser/IPL remains a consideration in clinical practice, though not observed in this cohort.

population.sample_type: Patients receiving laser/light treatments concurrently with systemic doxycycline

source_citation: Schilling, L. M., Halvorson, C. R., Weiss, R. A., Weiss, M. A., & Beasley, K. L. (2019). Safety of Combination Laser or Intense Pulsed Light Therapies and Doxycycline for the Treatment of Rosacea. Dermatologic Surgery, 45(12), 1578–1582. https://doi.org/10.1097/DSS.0000000000002009
adverse_events: No photosensitivity reactions observed in this cohort
Theoretical risk of phototoxicity with doxycycline + laser/IPL exists but was not manifested in this study
clinical_indication: Rosacea — combination treatment with systemic doxycycline and laser/IPL phototherapy

drugs_mentioned.name: doxycycline
drugs_mentioned.dental_relevance: Doxycycline is commonly prescribed in dentistry (periodontal therapy, prophylaxis). Theoretical photosensitivity risk when combined with laser or light-based dental treatments (e.g., diode laser, PBM/LLLT). This study found no photosensitivity reactions with PDL or IPL in dermatology, which may inform dental laser use, but direct extrapolation requires caution.
drugs_mentioned.laser_implication: Doxycycline carries a theoretical photosensitivity risk with laser and IPL treatments. This retrospective study found no photosensitivity reactions when combining doxycycline (standard or anti-inflammatory dose) with PDL (~595 nm) or IPL in rosacea patients. Clinicians using laser or PBM in patients on doxycycline should be aware of the theoretical risk but may proceed with appropriate monitoring based on available evidence.

perioperative_consideration: Patients on systemic doxycycline (standard or anti-inflammatory dose) undergoing laser or IPL treatments do not appear to have increased risk of photosensitivity reactions based on this retrospective cohort. However, the theoretical risk exists and clinicians should monitor patients and consider informing them of this possibility. No dose adjustment or treatment delay was required in this study.
uncertainties: Specific laser/IPL parameters (fluence, pulse duration, spot size) not reported — cannot assess dose-response relationship with photosensitivity risk.
IPL wavelength range not precisely specified in the abstract; assumed broadband visible-to-NIR spectrum.
Small retrospective sample (n=36) limits generalizability; rare photosensitivity events may not have been captured.
No control group; cannot determine whether combination therapy outcomes differ from monotherapy.
Doxycycline doses (standard vs. anti-inflammatory) not individually analyzed for differential photosensitivity risk.
Results are from a dermatology context (facial rosacea); direct extrapolation to dental laser applications requires caution.
Full text not available; some methodological details may be missing from abstract alone.

protocols.name: Terapia combinada doxiciclina + láser/IPL para rosácea — evaluación de seguridad fotosensibilizante
synonyms_es: fotosensibilidad por doxiciclina y luz pulsada
protocols.purpose: Evaluar la seguridad de combinar doxiciclina sistémica (dosis estándar o antiinflamatoria) con tratamientos de láser de colorante pulsado (PDL) o luz pulsada intensa (IPL) en pacientes con rosácea, con énfasis en el riesgo teórico de fotosensibilidad.

parameters_table.equipment_class: Láser de colorante pulsado (PDL) ~595 nm — uso en rosácea con doxiciclina concomitante
Luz pulsada intensa (IPL) — espectro visible-NIR broadband — uso en rosácea con doxiciclina concomitante

critical_notes: ⚠️ Este protocolo es de carácter retrospectivo/observacional. Los parámetros clínicos exactos (fluencia, duración de pulso, spot size) no fueron reportados en el estudio.
La doxiciclina tiene riesgo teórico de fotosensibilidad con luz láser e IPL; sin embargo, este estudio retrospectivo (n=36) no observó ninguna reacción adversa fotosensibilizante.
Tanto la dosis estándar como la dosis antiinflamatoria de doxiciclina fueron utilizadas sin diferencias en eventos adversos reportados.
El documento presenta esta combinación favorablemente; existen alternativas terapéuticas para rosácea sin el riesgo teórico de fotosensibilidad que pueden ser apropiadas según el caso clínico.
La extrapolación directa a procedimientos de láser dental (PBM, diodo, Er:YAG) en pacientes con doxiciclina requiere precaución adicional, ya que el contexto clínico difiere.
indications: Rosácea con componente vascular o eritematoso que requiere tratamiento combinado sistémico y fototerápico
Pacientes en tratamiento con doxiciclina (dosis estándar o antiinflamatoria) que requieren PDL o IPL para rosácea
contraindications: Hipersensibilidad conocida a doxiciclina o tetraciclinas
Historial documentado de reacciones de fotosensibilidad severa a tetraciclinas
postop_care: Monitorear al paciente para signos de fotosensibilidad cutánea tras cada sesión de láser/IPL
Instruir al paciente sobre protección solar adecuada durante el tratamiento con doxiciclina
protocols.evidence_level: Bajo — estudio retrospectivo sin grupo control, n=36. No permite establecer causalidad ni descartar eventos raros de fotosensibilidad.
